approach: sweep remaining 16 aced behavioral-leaf specs one node at a time
re-derive scenarios purely from drawn CFG (no golden-set reference exists)
additive self-clears; narrowing needs ratified re-open + Clearance
spec gates self-asserted within leash; impl gates surfaced to owner for ratification
dispatch per-node produce+judges to subagents to keep conductor context lean

why: RATIFIED LIVE by the owner, scoped to ONE frozen scenario in define-agent.feature: the impl-producer entry scenario asserted the pre-doctrine behavior of co-producing a separate eval suite (one eval per frozen scenario). Current SKILL.md line 10 and the M1-ratified sibling define-skill hold the opposite — the frozen .feature IS the verification, so the impl-producer authors no separate eval file — so a conformant build FAILS the frozen scenario today. Rewritten (not deleted) to assert the current contract, preserving its pair with the standalone-mode scenario so the suite still asserts no eval file is written in either mode. No other frozen scenario touched; the other four repairs are additive and self-clear.

Re-derived define-agent's scenario set from the CFG drawn off SKILL.md's real control flow (29 edges, bound 1:1 in the rebuilt four-section README). Four unbound edges got additive scenarios: the workflow-skill defer to define-skill, the model-carried-when-named positive companion, the tools-present-for-delegated positive companion, and a gate-role naming scenario flagging a non-verdict action name (eval-runner) — a positive discriminator killing the denylist mutant the bare-verb scenario survives. One frozen scenario was stale (co-produces an eval suite the implementation stopped writing) and was corrected under the recorded owner Clearance above. Cold aced-spec-validator ALIGNED: additive scenarios well-formed and edge-distinct, README binds every edge 1:1, co-produces scenario independently verified STALE-CONTRADICTS from SKILL.md. Mechanical checks green post-commit; the align-spec narrowing flag is the cleared re-open.

Owner raised the leash live: self-assert the impl gate on a cold impl-judge PASS with clean scope and no frozen-scenario rewrite; STOP for owner ratification on any Clearance/re-open (frozen narrowing) or a CHANGE verdict. Spec gates self-asserted throughout.

RATIFIED LIVE by the owner (this node carried a Clearance, so it stops per the raised leash). Cold aced-impl-judge re-derived all five changed/added scenarios' oracles independently by static inspection of SKILL.md: workflow-skill defer, model-carried-when-named, tools-present-for-delegated, the eval-runner gate-role flag, and the corrected impl-producer-writes-no-eval-suite scenario all PASS. Adversarial check on the eval-runner scenario confirmed the broadened positive-form rule (Name rule + B9) genuinely flags a non-verdict action name, not just the bare-verb denylist. Scope clean: only two SKILL.md lines changed (the gate-role naming broadening); SKILL.md:10's impl-producer eval doctrine was already correct on main, so only the spec was stale. No collateral break in the other 24 scenarios.

why: Re-derived the CFG from scripts/manage-skill-dirs.mts (operation dispatch + per-op guard chains). Suite was 25/28 CFG-complete; THREE genuine uncovered edges found and added as purely-additive scenarios: (1) induce rejects a ..-escape path (inducePatterns:157 — distinct from the nonexistent-path guard, which would NOT catch a ..-escape to a real external dir), (2) induce offers only the literal when segs.length<2 (no parent to generalize), (3) edit rejects a replacement that is a fixed default root (editSkillDir:136 — distinct from edit-to-malformed and the add-fixed guard). note: Cold spec-judge (architect obs): the frozen @rubric dimension name 'induces_and_previews_matched_skills' uses an A_and_B surface shape. NOT a rubric-structure hard-fail (binary max:1, no dishonest intermediate score; previewing entails inducing, so the _and_ joins a pipeline precondition to its deliverable, not two orthogonal virtues trading). Renaming to 'previews_matched_skills' would remove the surface form — but the dimension is inside a @frozen scenario, so it needs a ratified re-open + Clearance, not an additive. Defer.

note: Cold spec-judge (architect obs): report models trending-down as a fifth mutually-exclusive health class, but SKILL's own example (line 59, 'critical, trending down') treats trend as an OVERLAY flag on a pass-band status, not an exclusive class. True shape is 4 bands x a trend flag. Constructible overlap: latest 90% / previous 100% satisfies both the healthy band and the -10% trend. Today this does NOT fail pairwise-consistency (orthogonal axes, both Thens can hold), so the frozen suite is sound; but the CFG asserts an exclusivity SKILL doesn't honor, and SKILL never states band-vs-trend precedence. Reworking classify into a band+flag axis would rewrite the frozen classify scenarios (scn 8-12) = a re-open + Clearance, out of additive/auto-spec leash. Defer: owner to adjudicate whether to (a) model trend as a separate axis + add a composition scenario, or (b) have SKILL declare precedence.

All self-clear, feature stays @frozen, no re-open. Rebuilt README 4-section (23-row map 1:1, mermaid CFG, Fit:strong); added a threshold-resolution node + the once twin to the CFG. check:spec clean (had to reword the inline-bar scenario off the literal 'threshold' the suite-format guard bars as a score-leak -> 'inline pass bar'). Cold aced-spec-validator ALIGNED (3/3 lenses) on the pre-fix suite; its two named gaps closed by the two added scenarios; its third gap (per-shape collect distinction) filed as backlog.

note: Cold spec-judge (architect obs): run's per-shape COLLECT distinction is unspecified in the suite. SKILL 43-48 + the results-record schema (SKILL 89-114) say a @rubric case records per-dimension scores + total + pass/fail, a @trigger row records row/invoke/expected/pass, and a boolean records pass alone. The CFG collapses the three shapes into two branches (trigger-outline vs rubric/boolean) and no scenario binds the rubric-vs-boolean record-field difference. Adding it risks a double-barreled scenario (rubric AND boolean) and it sits close to aced-case-judge's return contract rather than run's routing, so deferred. If the persisted record shape is meant to be a frozen run guarantee, add per-shape scenarios (one each) rather than one folded scenario.

why: RATIFIED LIVE by the owner, scoped to add-scenario: re-open the frozen add-scenario.feature to re-align it from the RETIRED golden-set-directory model to the current .feature-append model. Commit 9f515205 ('read the frozen .feature suite directly across agents and skills') migrated all of ACED to 'the .feature is the single eval source' and updated add-scenario's SKILL.md to append Gherkin scenarios to the frozen .feature (trigger -> @trigger Examples row, deterministic behavior -> boolean, graded -> @rubric inline), but the frozen add-scenario.feature was never migrated and still specs writing NNN-<slug> case files under golden-set/ with a 4-part draft (expected-behaviors list / must-not-do list / scoring guide). The remaining golden-set/ dirs (aced-create-spec, sdd-orchestrator) are legacy fixtures. THREE frozen scenarios contradict the current SKILL and are rewritten (not deleted): 'the draft carries every required section' (scn 73), 'a confirmed case is written with the next sequence number' (scn 88), 'the first case in an empty golden set starts the sequence' (scn 93); scn 98 ('reports its path') refined off per-case-file wording. Surviving scenarios (triggering, suite-locate, transcript-decompose, must-not-do capture, layer inference/ambiguity/absent, draft confirm/revise) are untouched. Owner ratifies the impl gate live after the cold judges pass (node carries a Clearance, so it stops per the auto-all leash).

Re-derived add-scenario's CFG from the CURRENT plugins/aced/skills/add-scenario/SKILL.md (.feature-append model post-9f515205: route -> locate suite -> gather(transcript decompose | must-not-do guard | description) -> determine layer(trigger/behavior/quality table, ambiguous->ask, absent->flag) -> scaffold by layer shape(trigger Examples row | boolean | graded scoring-guide inline) -> confirm-before-write -> append to .feature in lifecycle section, keep @frozen self-clears, run check-suite, report + suggest run). Re-open executed under the owner Clearance recorded above: FOUR frozen scenarios that specced the retired golden-set/NNN-slug model rewritten (draft-is-a-tagged-Gherkin-scenario; confirmed-case-appended-to-.feature; appending-keeps-@frozen; written-case-reports-added-scenario), the .feature header + all residual 'golden set' vocabulary scrubbed to the append model. SIX additive scenarios: three scaffold shapes (trigger row / boolean / graded scoring-guide inline), check-suite-after-write, and behavior + quality layer inference (completing the layer-inference enumeration the cold spec-judge flagged as unbound). Suite 17->24. check:spec: only align-spec flags the ratified re-open narrowing (4 removed/8 added mechanical diff; clears against HEAD once committed); check-suite map 1:1 green, all else green. Cold aced-spec-validator FIRST verdict NOT-ALIGNED on two gaps (half-migrated .feature header/vocab; unbound behavior/quality layer edges); both fixed; re-verified ALIGNED (re-open justified AND complete, no residual). Spec gate self-asserted within leash (the Clearance is the owner's spec-side ratification).

Owner-flagged during add-scenario ratification: the ACED skills hardcoded the RETIRED suite location artifacts/specs/<feature-name>/ (holding eval.md + <feature-name>.feature + results/). Owner decision (live): the canonical model is EVERYTHING IN THE PROJECT SPEC — a target's behavioral-leaf node under .agents/specs/<project>/.../<node>/ holds the frozen <node>.feature, a colocated eval.md (subject + run policy, two-level shape unchanged), and results/; artifacts/specs/ is retired; ACED discovers the node through the SDD spec tree (the node's eval.md names the subject). Swept the location/binding wording across 7 skills + 1 spec-README (run, add-scenario, compare, report[SKILL+README], improve, aced-impl-producer SKILLs; eval-run/report/README.md). LOCATION-ONLY: no scenario logic, rubric/threshold semantics, eval.md policy fields, layer routing, or .feature-append behavior touched. The frozen suites are PATH-NEUTRAL (they say 'locate the suite'/'reads target and scoring bar' with no path), so this is a conformance-PRESERVING impl/docs fix, NOT a re-open. grep artifacts/specs across plugins/aced/skills is now zero. Cold impl-judge re-verified add-scenario's two LOCATE scenarios PASS against the corrected step (the step my node-9 impl-judge originally didn't cover — the gap the owner caught). check:spec green.

note: BROADER golden-set migration is incomplete beyond the location fix (out of scope for the owner-approved location sweep; several parts need re-opens and/or a Quill docs node). Residual: (1) glossary.md:25 still defines 'eval suite' as 'eval.md ... plus its golden-set/'; (2) FROZEN suites still carry 'golden-set' vocabulary as routing near-miss/deferral text — define-agent.feature:36, define-skill.feature:52/58, define-governance.feature:31, scenario-writer.feature:29/91, skillify.feature:27/37, contribute-skill.feature:29/48, workflows/eval-loop.feature:8 (rewrites would be Clearance-bound re-opens); (3) artifacts/specs/*/ dirs still physically exist with legacy golden-set/ + eval.md (e.g. aced-create-spec, sdd-orchestrator) — the eval.md subject+policy must migrate INTO each target's project-spec node dir per the new model, and the golden-set/ fixtures retired; (4) prior followups already track ~140 golden-set/*.md fixtures + docs on the retired 1-5 scalar (github-263-op6-m3) and 'migrate remaining 6 golden-set suites' (aced-suite-direct). Recommend a dedicated migration mission after the M2 sweep.

Frozen suite covered 28 edges; 4 ADDITIVE scenarios re-derived from CFG gaps: (1) leading-And-does-not-inherit-across-scenario-boundary (the lastKeyword=null reset at parse:104 — the pre-existing orphan-And scenario is BLIND to removing that reset since it opens the first scenario); (2) malformed --row fails closed (main:252-258 arg guard, distinct from the RowOutOfRange table check); (3) json emits situation regrouped by keyword (formatJson path); (4) json withholds the answer key like the brief (second leak surface, positive companion = #3). Auto-all: additive-only, no frozen rewrite, clean scope -> self-assert on cold impl-judge PASS. The engine .mts was UNCHANGED this mission (suite re-derived from existing control flow). Cold aced-impl-judge re-derived all 4 added scenarios' oracles and PASSED each against the .mts + a backing node:test: #1 leading-And reset (parse:104, test:511, ablation-confirmed non-vacuous); #2 malformed --row guard (main:252-258, tests:882/1395); #3 json regroup (formatJson + given/when, test:1271); #4 json withholds answer key (tests:285/470). 88/88 tests pass. The impl-judge ran ablation controls and surfaced that scenario #2's backing tests were UNDER-BOUND (asserted only exit!=0 + empty stdout, which a downstream allRows[NaN] crash also satisfies — ablating the main guard left them green).
